reviews.review_msg: Marc as a human is SUPER kind & respectful. However, his skills as a TA are poor to non existent. Did not give part marks on the midterm, did not show us them in tut when prof said he would, came to lecture late with coffee spilled on them, and provided no clarity within marking. Tutorials consisted of him rambling about his life and experiences.

He never got to finish teaching the class as he left in week 10, but I liked him. He was available in office hours and after lectures to explain content and the weekly quizzes in tutorial were not difficult if you read the chapter. The TA Marcel was the highlight as he was very accessible through email and led great tutorials.

Worst upper div course taken thus far, incredibly unclear with how exams and papers will be graded, in addition to what will be on exams in the first place. Weekly readings and tutorial questions split between online readings and 2 textbooks total over 100 pages for some weeks alone, does not cover a lot of test materials in lecture due to time :(

Close to 100 pages of reading each week from two textbooks that were either quizzed on or on the midterm/exam.Course was extremely disorganized (final exam content was ambiguous). Professor assigned far too many discussion questions (10-25) for tutorial to the point that we would never finish them (although all were examinable).

This was one of the hardest classes I've ever taken in Crim. 2 textbooks, one for weekly quizzes worth 30% and one for exams/discussion.Tutorials discuss questions from the other textbook, also on exams. Additional to the 2 textbooks, there are also articles assigned which are included on exams. If you dont LOVE philosophy, avoid this class 100%
Way too much reading and the quizzes are impossible. Take 335 instead for your legal certificate, this isn't worth it

There's ALOT of reading for this class. There are weekly quizzes coming from a separate textbook that isn't tested on the midterm/final (therefore, he hardly touches on this in class so you have to prepare for it yourself). The weekly quizzes are weighed the heaviest part of your overall mark. The term paper is also very hard.
He's an amazing prof. Crim 338 is a tough course. Its philosophy and is difficult for a typical criminology student, like me.  However, MacAlister is great and always give interesting lectures.

In addition to reading a chapter from the Hart book every week for the weekly quizzes (worth 30%) there is a significant amount of readings for the discussion questions every week (the discussion questions are directly taken and put on midterm so also important). The term paper for the class is 6-8 pages and once again requires you to read 80pgs

You definitley have to put in some effort. There are 10 quizzes all 6 multiple choice questions worth 30% of your grade. The questions come from the weekly assigned chapters in another small book Midterm was all short answer questions that came from the list of "discussion" questions. Final was all M/C.  Paper is short but requires a lot of reading
Interesting lecturer. Very knowledgable.
David is great prof...always well prepared and is able to make an abstract subject interesting. I learned so much from him, definitely one of the best.
Crim 338 is awesome, DO THE HOMEWORK QUESTIONS, Lorinda (ta) AWESOME....notes available online so you can actually listen to the PROF.I would take a course with him again!

Prof MacAlister An AMAZING Prof highly recommended! One of the best AT SFU. He is extremely well prepared & genuinely wants students to do well! Very insightful, compelling and happy to help with all questions! Crim 338 is one of my favorite classes! I got an A, impart due to his thorough notes and willingness to help!

I enjoyed crim 338, it was my first course at SFU. Lorinda is an excellent TA. There are a lot of readings but nothing to be frightened about, just do and keep up with the homework questions and you will do fine for everything..The prof did not have offensive humor, notes are made available online..come to class and tut and try, you'll do fine

I agree with the most recent rating. I also resent the amount of humour put toward a class that deals with some very serious subject matter. I am amazed at the other comments that praise his humour.Humour is never something that I would include in my analysis of a professor's teaching ability.

I do not agree that David is good Professor. I had him at Kwantlen and he still teaches as if at that level. I am resentful of the amount of humour that he puts toward a very serious subject matter. I'm not paying for a comedian.

Margaret is a nice person, however the course itself is just brutal! Her exams were quite hard and she expected a lot even though the material was very difficult to understand. This course is not about memorizing, you need to apply your knowledge and really understand the concepts and theories. mt and final are open book, m/c and essay.
I put in the normal amount of work and did not do well. I usually am an above average student and this was not the case in all THREE classes I have taken with her. 317 last year and 335/338 this sems

The negative reviews are exaggerated. Amy is not the best prof but she is not bad at all. There is nothing to prevent you from doing well if you actually study and ask questions if you're confused. The course content is hard, but she posts lecture recordings AND gave the opportunity for extra credit. I put in a normal amount of work and got an A.

Amy is a nice prof, allows cheat sheets and extra credit. However, she says that lecture is a class discussion but tends to talk about things that don't really make sense. Beware, her quizzes and midterm are based on class discussion and there is a lot of reading u must do.
She is a really harsh marker! GPA killer

I don't know why there are so many negative comments about Amy! Her lectures are so interesting. You just need to pay attention to what she's saying during the lecture to fully understand the readings. The course content itself is hard. But she posts lecture recordings, so you can always go back and review them. I recommend taking classes with Amy.

I will never forget the amount of stress this class caused me. She is an extremely hard grader. I struggled so much in this class. I wish I never took this class with her, my GPA is going to be ruined. Please take any prof besides her. She assigns so many confusing readings and expect you to understand them all in depth. The exams are so hard too.

Amy tries to make us look at things more critically, and I definitely learned to be more attentive to detail by taking this class, but if English is not your first language or you get distracted easily, her classes are difficult to follow. The slides are useless, she mostly talks (FAST) and that's what your notes will be. Grading can be harsh too.

Amy sucked at teaching this course. she was all over the place. only good thing was that some cases overlapped from 335 so those were easy to understand but otherwise I would not recommend taking this course.

I have taken several classes with Professor Conroy, and her classes are very engaging and I recommend attending the lectures in-person for discussions! She is open to concerns and questions, and provides clear instructions on how to reach the grade you desire. Stay caught up with the readings and lectures if you want to do well and ask questions!

I want to cry. I have never been this stressed in all of my years at SFU. Her exams and quizzes are incredibly difficult. She words them in such confusing ways, it's like she wants her students to not do well. Save yourself the stress and do not take any class with her. I wish I listened to the reviews on here.

TA SAM WAS THE BEST. This is the most stressed I have ever been in a semester. gives us only 4 days to work on a take home exam which is worth so much of our final grade AND cumulative! some of us cannot book off work and need more than 4 days to complete an exam. took two classes with her this semester and I am honestly fried

Probably the worst professor of my undergrad. I had to take her 3 times so it's not just one course. She is clearly ideologically possessed and is dismissive of challenges to what she says. She will rarely answer a question honestly and will ridicule you or distract you by changing the subject or by using jargon. Also, her slides are useless. 0/10.

I think Amy gets a bad rep from students. She definitely cares about students and lets the final be take home and you can have a cheatsheet during the midterm which is a lifesaver. However, she can be a difficult grader on the first assignment and expect you to know some details you may think are a bit unfair.

The course content is interesting, however, Amy makes it impossible to demonstrate what you learned. The exam was one hour with 15 mc and 20 written where she expected paragraph answers to the written question (the questions themselves were long so it took a minute to even READ the question). I do NOT recommend if you care about your grades.

Exams really easy (95% based on lecture, with some stupid specific Q's), but unclear grading criteria on assignment. Make sure you reply to many people for tutorials or you'll lose many marks. Don't bother with textbook other than to skim for context. Lectures were <50 minutes/week. Didn't get much out of the class. TA was not great either.

Amy only provides you pre-recorded lectures where she speaks so illiterately fast that it makes it impossible to take accurate notes. She will test you on the tiniest details she verbally mentions in those recordings since there is ZERO info on her slides, meanwhile I cannot grasp a word she is saying. She has a horrible teaching system.
